title: xanthorrhoea media
intro: Xanthorrhoea media, also known as the black boy or red grass tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Xanthorrhoeaceae, native to eastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and skin irritations, with its health benefits including soothing and calming the body. The herbal preparations of Xanthorrhoea media include tea, extracts, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
